content.content:
1	//go:build linux
2	
3	package nftables
4	
5	import (
6		"errors"
7		"flag"
8		"fmt"
9		"os"
10		"path/filepath"
11		"strings"
12		"testing"
13	
14		"github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp"
15		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
16	
17		"github.com/influxdata/telegraf"
18		"github.com/influxdata/telegraf/config"
19		"github.com/influxdata/telegraf/plugins/inputs"
20		"github.com/influxdata/telegraf/plugins/parsers/influx"
21		"github.com/influxdata/telegraf/testutil"
22	)
23	
24	func TestCases(t *testing.T) {
25		// Get all directories in testdata
26		folders, err := os.ReadDir("testcases")
27		require.NoError(t, err)
28	
29		// Register the plugin
30		inputs.Add("nftables", func() telegraf.Input {
31			return &Nftables{}
32		})
33	
34		// Prepare the influx parser for expectations
35		parser := &influx.Parser{}
36		require.NoError(t, parser.Init())
37	
38		for _, f := range folders {
39			// Only handle folders
40			if !f.IsDir() {
41				continue
42			}
43			testcasePath := filepath.Join("testcases", f.Name())
44			configFilename := filepath.Join(testcasePath, "telegraf.conf")
45			expectedFilename := filepath.Join(testcasePath, "expected.out")
46			expectedErrorFilename := filepath.Join(testcasePath, "expected.err")
47	
48			// Compare options
49			options := []cmp.Option{
50				testutil.IgnoreTime(),
51				testutil.SortMetrics(),
52			}
53	
54			t.Run(f.Name(), func(t *testing.T) {
55				var expected []telegraf.Metric
56				if _, err := os.Stat(expectedFilename); err == nil {
57					var err error
58					expected, err = testutil.ParseMetricsFromFile(expectedFilename, parser)
59					require.NoError(t, err)
60				}
61	
62				// Read the expected output if any
63				var expectedErrors []string
64				if _, err := os.Stat(expectedErrorFilename); err == nil {
65					var err error
66					expectedErrors, err = testutil.ParseLinesFromFile(expectedErrorFilename)
67					require.NoError(t, err)
68					require.NotEmpty(t, expectedErrors)
69				}
70	
71				// Determine the executable name of the test-binary used to mock the
72				// nft command. See TestMain
73				exe, err := os.Executable()
74				require.NoError(t, err)
75	
76				// Configure the plugin
77				cfg := config.NewConfig()
78				require.NoError(t, cfg.LoadConfig(configFilename))
79				require.Len(t, cfg.Inputs, 1)
80	
81				// Mock the nft executable
82				plugin := cfg.Inputs[0].Input.(*Nftables)
83				plugin.Binary = exe
84				require.NoError(t, plugin.Init())
85				plugin.args = append([]string{"--mock", "--testcase", testcasePath}, plugin.args...)
86	
87				// Gather the metrics and compare the output
88				var acc testutil.Accumulator
89				require.NoError(t, plugin.Gather(&acc))
90				if len(acc.Errors) > 0 {
91					actualErrorMsgs := make([]string, 0, len(acc.Errors))
92					for _, err := range acc.Errors {
93						actualErrorMsgs = append(actualErrorMsgs, err.Error())
94					}
95					require.ElementsMatch(t, actualErrorMsgs, expectedErrors)
96				}
97				testutil.RequireMetricsEqual(t, expected, acc.GetTelegrafMetrics(), options...)
98			})
99		}
100	}
101	
102	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
103		// Mimic the nft command line arguments
104		var mock, jsonMode bool
105		var testcase string
106		flag.BoolVar(&mock, "mock", false, "run test as mock")
107		flag.StringVar(&testcase, "testcase", "", "path to the test directory")
108		flag.BoolVar(&jsonMode, "json", false, "output as JSON")
109		flag.Parse()
110	
111		if !mock {
112			// Run the normal test mode
113			os.Exit(m.Run())
114		}
115	
116		// Run as a mock program
117		if !jsonMode {
118			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "JSON mode not set")
119			os.Exit(1)
120		}
121	
122		args := flag.Args()
123		if nargs := len(args); nargs != 3 {
124			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "invalid number of arguments, expected 3 got %d\n", nargs)
125			os.Exit(1)
126		}
127		if args[0] != "list" {
128			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "expected \"list\" command got %q\n", args[0])
129			os.Exit(1)
130		}
131		if args[1] != "table" {
132			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "expected \"list\" command got %q\n", args[0])
133			os.Exit(1)
134		}
135	
136		filename := filepath.Join(testcase, "table_"+args[2]+".json")
137		buf, err := os.ReadFile(filename)
138		if err != nil {
139			if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
140				fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Error: No such file or directory")
141				fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "list table", args[1])
142				fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "          ", strings.Repeat("^", len(args[1])))
143			} else {
144				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "reading file %q failed: %v", filename, err)
145			}
146			os.Exit(1)
147		}
148		// This mimics the command output, do not remove!
149		fmt.Print(string(buf))
150		os.Exit(0)
151	}
152	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.text:
Implemented the nftables set unmarshalling fix.

What changed:
- Updated `plugins/inputs/nftables/types.go`
- Changed `namedSet.Elem` from `[]elem` to `[]json.RawMessage`
- Removed the empty `elem` struct

Why this fixes the bug:
- The plugin only needs `len(set.Elem)` to count set entries.
- `json.RawMessage` allows each set element to be any valid JSON value, including:
  - strings like `"10.47.55.250"`
  - objects like `{ "elem": { "val": "::1", "expires": 99 } }`
- This prevents unmarshalling failures for predefined nftables sets whose `elem` array contains plain strings.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression testcase reproducing the reported string-element set.
- Confirmed it failed before the fix with:
  - `json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field namedSet.elem of type nftables.elem`
- Confirmed it passed after the fix.
- Deleted the temporary testcase as requested.
- Ran the nftables plugin test suite successfully:

```text
go test ./plugins/inputs/nftables
ok  	github.com/influxdata/telegraf/plugins/inputs/nftables
```

No commit was created.

result:
Implemented the nftables set unmarshalling fix.

What changed:
- Updated `plugins/inputs/nftables/types.go`
- Changed `namedSet.Elem` from `[]elem` to `[]json.RawMessage`
- Removed the empty `elem` struct

Why this fixes the bug:
- The plugin only needs `len(set.Elem)` to count set entries.
- `json.RawMessage` allows each set element to be any valid JSON value, including:
  - strings like `"10.47.55.250"`
  - objects like `{ "elem": { "val": "::1", "expires": 99 } }`
- This prevents unmarshalling failures for predefined nftables sets whose `elem` array contains plain strings.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression testcase reproducing the reported string-element set.
- Confirmed it failed before the fix with:
  - `json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field namedSet.elem of type nftables.elem`
- Confirmed it passed after the fix.
- Deleted the temporary testcase as requested.
- Ran the nftables plugin test suite successfully:

```text
go test ./plugins/inputs/nftables
ok  	github.com/influxdata/telegraf/plugins/inputs/nftables
```

No commit was created.
